lanes.label: Patient backend
lanes.summary: Projection owners for patient home, request detail, child context, records, and communications.

lanes.label: Patient frontend
lanes.summary: Patient account routes consume backend projections and publish browser proof without route-local truth.

lanes.label: Support backend
lanes.summary: Support lineage, ticket workspace, controlled repair, delivery, replay, and fallback foundations.

lanes.label: Support frontend
lanes.summary: Staff entry, support ticket shell, masking, read-only fallback, and contextual assist surfaces.

tasks.taskName: Patient spotlight decision projection and quiet home logic
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not own request detail, typed action routing, more-info, record, communications, or support ticket semantics.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: Home alias resolution and spotlight use-window proofs must land; gap HOME closed or explicitly reconciled.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Fixture-backed request, status, reachability, and identity signals now; richer records/messages/support candidates enter later through the same ranking rules.

tasks.taskName: Request browsing, detail, lineage, and typed patient action routing projections
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not decide home spotlight ownership, callback truth, record parity, communications chronology, or support lineage ownership.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: List, detail, downstream, return bundle, action routing, settlement, and safety interruption parity must agree with frontend task 215.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Use current canonical request/status/continuity outputs now; future child projections plug into downstream placeholders and typed routing only.

tasks.taskName: More-info response thread, callback status, reachability, and contact repair projections
sourceSections: patient-account-and-communications-blueprint.md#Callback status, contact repair, and consent checkpoint contract
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not replace request detail/action routing or own communications timeline chronology beyond callback and reply child truth.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: REQUEST_CONTEXT gap closed; blocker-first dominance proven with 216 and communications placeholders.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Simulator and fixture reachability/callback chains now; live provider callback evidence stays actual-later through Phase 2 carry-forward.

tasks.taskName: Health record projection and record artifact parity witness
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not invent request status, communications visibility, or support artifact authority; record summaries remain subordinate to source artifacts.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: Record parity witness must be present before charts, downloads, previews, or follow-up actions go live.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Fixture artifact and release posture now; production clinical record integrations and live downloads remain actual-later.

tasks.taskName: Communications timeline and message/callback visibility rules
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not own support ticket chronology, controlled resend, record artifact parity, or request action settlement.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: COMMUNICATIONS gap closed; patient message rows, thread mastheads, callback cards, reminders, and receipts agree.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Simulator-backed dispatch, receipt, and callback facts now; live provider evidence remains actual-later.

tasks.taskName: Frontend patient home, requests, and request detail routes
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not recompute backend projection truth, add dashboard filler, or arm route-local CTAs outside PatientNextActionProjection.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: Playwright proof for home/list/detail; governed placeholders for every missing sibling projection; no dashboard drift.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Mock projection payloads can drive UI states now; later real APIs must preserve identical typed projection consumption.

tasks.taskName: Frontend more-info, callback status, and contact repair views
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not detach more-info into a standalone app or infer callback timers locally.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: REQUEST_CONTEXT and COMMUNICATIONS seams reconciled; Playwright covers blocker-first, confirmation, recovery, and read-only states.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Fixture thread and repair payloads now; live delivery and callback evidence later through the same projections.

tasks.taskName: Frontend health record and communications timeline views
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not make charts or previews authoritative, hide blocked communications, or invent record interpretation locally.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: RECORDS and COMMUNICATIONS seams closed or placeholders governed; Playwright covers parity fallback and visibility placeholders.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Fixture records and conversation clusters now; production documents and live message evidence later.

tasks.taskName: Support lineage binding, ticket projection, and subject history queries
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not replace patient request, message, identity, callback, artifact, or communications truth with support-local copies.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: SUPPORT_LINEAGE gap closed; support ticket shell and subject context cite lineage, artifact, mask scope, and disclosure records.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Fixture support tickets and masked subject summaries now; live support data migration later.

tasks.taskName: Controlled resend, delivery repair, and support replay foundations
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not rewrite provider adapters, claim live provider proof, or create second timeline truth through resend or replay.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: SUPPORT_REPAIR_REPLAY gap closed; duplicate clicks, retries, replay checkpoints, release, restore, and read-only fallback are idempotent and evidence-bound.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Simulator-backed delivery and replay evidence now; live provider webhook proof later.

tasks.taskName: Staff start-of-day operations and support entry surfaces
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not turn staff entry into a KPI dashboard or invent deeper ticket semantics beyond governed placeholders.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: Playwright proves quiet workbench, operations entry, support inbox entry, placeholders, reduced motion, and no support ownership creep.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Fixture entry projections now; live operations publication and queue data later through the same route contracts.

tasks.taskName: Support workspace shell and omnichannel ticket views
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not own masking-depth, read-only fallback depth, or backend mutation semantics; may only render bounded placeholders for 222 child surfaces.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: Playwright proves ticket-centric same shell, chronology parity, action-entry boundaries, degraded states, and anchor preservation.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Fixture tickets and timelines now; production support integrations later.

tasks.taskName: Support masking, read-only fallback, contextual knowledge, observe, and replay panels
tasks.forbiddenOverlap: May not fork the support shell anatomy, widen disclosure locally, or treat replay/observe state as live writable authority.
tasks.mergeGateRequirements: Playwright proves masking preserves chronology, read-only fallback keeps anchors, knowledge assists are bounded, and replay/observe posture is visible.
tasks.mockNowVsActualLaterAssumptions: Fixture masking, knowledge, observe, and replay states now; live disclosure and replay sessions later.

mergeGates.label: Every task publishes required contracts
mergeGates.requirement: Tasks 210-222 must each publish their promised docs, data, validator, and Playwright or backend proof before seq_223 integrates the block.

mergeGates.label: Shared interfaces have one owner
mergeGates.requirement: Every interface in the shared registry has exactly one authoritative owner and all consumers bind through that owner or an explicit gap artifact.

mergeGates.label: Phase 2 truth is consumed
mergeGates.requirement: Identity, session, release/trust, same-shell, return, request, duplicate, masking, and replay laws remain frozen and are not redefined by patient or support tasks.

mergeGates.label: No dashboard or route-local drift
mergeGates.requirement: Patient home stays quiet and projection-owned; staff/support entry stays workbench-owned; no route may recompute action, visibility, or status truth from local joins.

mergeGates.label: Parallel gaps are explicit and resolved
mergeGates.requirement: Every missing sibling seam is represented by a PARALLEL_INTERFACE_GAP_CROSSCUTTING artifact and is either closed by the owning task or carried into 223 as a named blocker.

mergeGates.label: Mock-now and actual-later stay separated
mergeGates.requirement: Simulator, fixture, and board evidence may open the block; live provider, live credential, clinical-safety, DSPT, and production operational evidence remain actual-later.

mergeGates.label: Board agrees with registry
mergeGates.requirement: The browser gate board, track matrix, merge gate strip, seam ribbon, ownership conflict panel, and mock/actual boundary map agree exactly with machine-readable artifacts.

forbiddenOverlaps.title: Home spotlight cannot own request action semantics
forbiddenOverlaps.ownerBoundary: par_210 owns spotlight selection; par_211 owns request action routing and settlement.
forbiddenOverlaps.risk: Two dominant patient CTAs or a home card that bypasses typed action routing.
forbiddenOverlaps.mergeCheck: Patient home Playwright proof must show the spotlight CTA binds to PatientNextActionProjection or renders read-only.

forbiddenOverlaps.title: Request detail cannot hide missing child context
forbiddenOverlaps.ownerBoundary: par_211 owns shell/detail placeholders; par_212, par_213, and par_214 own child truth.
forbiddenOverlaps.risk: More-info, callback, record, or message work disappears instead of degrading to governed placeholders.
forbiddenOverlaps.mergeCheck: Request downstream projections must enumerate every missing child with a placeholder or gap artifact.

forbiddenOverlaps.title: Records and communications cannot redefine status
forbiddenOverlaps.ownerBoundary: par_213 owns source-artifact parity; par_214 owns visibility/receipt chronology; neither owns canonical request status.
forbiddenOverlaps.risk: Record summaries, callback cards, or receipt copy contradict request list/detail state.
forbiddenOverlaps.mergeCheck: Lineage and receipt parity evidence must bind to PatientRequestLineageProjection and authoritative settlement refs.

forbiddenOverlaps.title: Frontend routes cannot compose truth locally
forbiddenOverlaps.ownerBoundary: par_215 to par_217 consume typed patient projections; par_220 to par_222 consume typed support projections.
forbiddenOverlaps.risk: Browser state, local joins, optimistic timers, or dashboard widgets become de facto domain logic.
forbiddenOverlaps.mergeCheck: Playwright and validators must assert projection payload parity for every browser state.

forbiddenOverlaps.title: Support cannot become a second system of record
forbiddenOverlaps.ownerBoundary: par_218 frames support tickets over lineage; patient request, message, callback, identity, and artifact truths stay upstream.
forbiddenOverlaps.risk: Ticket-local copies diverge from patient-visible truth or mask scope.
forbiddenOverlaps.mergeCheck: Support projections cite SupportLineageBinding, SupportLineageArtifactBinding, and disclosure records for every widened context.

forbiddenOverlaps.title: Repair and replay cannot fork external effects
forbiddenOverlaps.ownerBoundary: par_219 owns controlled resend, repair, replay checkpoint, release, and restore settlement; frontend tasks render only those states.
forbiddenOverlaps.risk: Duplicate operator clicks or provider retries create second external side effects or timeline truth.
forbiddenOverlaps.mergeCheck: Support action settlement and replay release must prove idempotency, evidence freeze, live-restore checks, and read-only fallback.

continuityLaws.label: Identity-binding truth
continuityLaws.rule: No patient or support surface may reinterpret authenticated subject, identity hold, wrong-patient repair, or binding supersession outside the Phase 2 binding outputs.

continuityLaws.label: Session truth
continuityLaws.rule: Local session, logout, expiry, state, nonce, and same-shell recovery remain owned by the Phase 2 session governor.

continuityLaws.label: Release and trust freezes
continuityLaws.rule: Writable posture requires route intent, release freeze, channel release posture, and assurance-slice trust to remain live for the governing object.

continuityLaws.label: Same-shell continuity
continuityLaws.rule: Home, request detail, more-info, records, messages, support ticket, observe, and replay routes preserve shell and selected anchor when the continuity tuple is still valid.

continuityLaws.label: Return contracts
continuityLaws.rule: Patient and support child routes return through typed return bundles or continuity evidence, not browser history alone.

continuityLaws.label: Canonical request and duplicate truth
continuityLaws.rule: Request lineage, duplicate follow-up, late evidence, and re-safety remain canonical request truth and may only be consumed through the published projections.

continuityLaws.label: Contact and reachability truth
continuityLaws.rule: Reachability, contact repair, consent, callback, and delivery posture are blocker-first and may not be bypassed by stale optimistic actions.

continuityLaws.label: Masking and replay truth
continuityLaws.rule: Support summary, replay, observe, and read-only fallback preserve chronology and mask scope without becoming a second system of record.

driftControls: dashboard drift blocked by PatientSpotlightDecisionProjection and PatientQuietHomeDecision ownership
route-local action drift blocked by PatientActionRoutingProjection and PatientActionSettlementProjection
support ownership creep blocked by SupportLineageBinding and SupportLineageArtifactBinding
